content.problemStatement:
Call a number stepping if adjacent digits, as well as the first and last digits, differ by one. How many n-digit base 11 stepping numbers are there? Give your answer modulo 4294967143.


For example, 8789A9 is a 6-digit base 11 stepping number. 9A0A and 234 are not stepping.
constraints: Base is fixed to 11, digits are 0-9 and A (value 10).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N, the length of the number.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of N-digit base-11 stepping numbers modulo 4294967143.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, the number of N-digit base-11 stepping numbers is calculated and printed.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the number of i-digit stepping numbers ending with digit j. We start by initializing the base case where i = 1. Then, for each i from 2 to N, we calculate dp[i][j] by considering all possible previous digits that can lead to j. We use modular arithmetic to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * 11) per test case, O(T * N * 11) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * 11) — the dominant structure is the 2D array dp.
